data.title: Sprint orchestrator bypasses dso:doc-writer for documentation stories
data.description:
## Summary

When sprint executes documentation stories (story type targeting CLAUDE.md, design-notes.md, KNOWN-ISSUES.md), the orchestrator implements the changes directly instead of delegating to the dso:doc-writer sub-agent as intended.

## Observed behavior
The sprint orchestrator read task descriptions and directly edited CLAUDE.md, created .claude/design-notes.md, and updated .claude/docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md without invoking the doc-writer agent.

## Expected behavior
Documentation stories should dispatch dso:doc-writer (as noted in CLAUDE.md: 'dso:doc-writer | sonnet | /dso:sprint (doc stories)') to ensure consistent doc quality, proper 4-tier schema enforcement, and agent-level review.

## Impact
Documentation quality is not enforced by the doc-writer agent's 4-tier schema. The orchestrator may miss doc conventions or produce docs not aligned with the doc-writer quality standard.

## Fix needed
Sprint SKILL.md should include guidance that when a story is classified as a documentation story (story touching .claude/docs/, CLAUDE.md, design-notes.md, or KNOWN-ISSUES.md), the orchestrator must dispatch dso:doc-writer instead of writing directly.
